reasoning: ADI's stock plunged from ~200 to 162 in early April due to China retaliatory tariffs, rebounding to 176.47 by April 11. Short-term indicators, including historical reversion patterns on such crashes, suggest an oversold bounce is highly probable in the coming weeks (yfinance tool models an initial P=0.60). However, the medium-term outlook is clouded by anticipated 10-20% downward revisions in sector guidance and possible recessionary risks. Because of this structural uncertainty around earnings impacts (the next earnings report is in late May), long-term conviction fades. In accordance with forecasting principles regarding forecast horizon, probabilities are pulled closer to 0.50 for the more distant resolution dates, transitioning from an initial 0.60 to 0.52.
